label: PASS
rationale: Admin SP Revert was invoked in a read-write Admin SP session and returned SUCCESS with an empty result list, matching the method signature and mandatory Admin SP support.

label: FAIL
rationale: Revert succeeded but returned a non-empty result list; the documented signature is SPObjectUID.Revert[] => [] and Admin SP Revert should have an empty result list.

label: FAIL
rationale: The immediately preceding Admin SP session was opened with Write=0, but Revert operates within a read-write session to the Admin SP, so SUCCESS is not protocol-compliant here.

label: FAIL
rationale: After successful Revert on the Admin SP object, the TPer must abort the session immediately after reporting status; accepting a subsequent Set in that session is non-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: SID had previously authenticated and the feature descriptor behavior was 0x00, so Admin SP Revert must set C_PIN_SID to C_PIN_MSID; StartSession with MSID succeeding is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: After successful Admin SP Revert with SID previously authenticated and behavior 0x00, C_PIN_SID should become C_PIN_MSID, so authenticating with the old changed PIN should not succeed.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Locking SP had been activated before Admin SP Revert, so it was not Manufactured-Inactive; successful Admin SP Revert must remove user data and eradicate media encryption keys, making the old written pattern unavailable.
